refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain analysis, obstacle analysis, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, avoiding any implementation details or combined requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze and report on the terrain characteristics of the worksite area independently of other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate safety and operational policy constraints into the terrain analysis
Provide an interface to share the generated worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area

potential_sources: Integration of safety policies tightly coupled with terrain modeling
Sharing interface dependencies on specific planning/execution modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently of policy integration and interface sharing, supporting modularity and reducing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that safety and operational policies can be integrated downstream without impacting terrain modeling
Risk that incomplete feature extraction may limit model usefulness for planning/execution
Assumes agreed set of site features is well-defined and stable
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating feature extraction and policy integration into distinct DPs to further reduce coupling and clarify responsibilities.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions, including terrain modeling and digital map generation for the worksite area.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle and other sources to build the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters related to terrain model generation and updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends the generated digital terrain model data to other modules or external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion since data inputs and outputs are well defined through telemetry and network link.
Vehicle primarily handles physical operations; terrain modeling would require extensive data transfer and processing outside its scope, causing high diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and command; terrain modeling is not central and would cause significant diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; terrain modeling is unrelated and would cause very high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP because it centralizes data processing and terrain-related computations, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module.
Network link reliability may affect distribution of terrain model data to other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain' by specifying a concrete method to analyze the terrain through digital modeling.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is well-contained with a high cohesion score (9) and low diffusion score (1), indicating that the DP is effectively centralized within a single implementation module. External interfaces are clearly defined, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the availability of the generated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions, as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly aligns with the FR and DP, with well-defined interfaces and minimal overlap with other modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear, direct satisfaction of the FR by the DP, effective containment and low diffusion risk in the IM, and feasible verification and clear ownership alignment. There are no recommended revisions and no diffusion risks or verification issues. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here to maintain clarity and control rather than adding complexity unnecessarily.

assumptions: The DP fully and adequately satisfies the FR as stated.
The IM module encapsulates the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion, ensuring architectural soundness.
Verification methods are sufficient and feasible for acceptance criteria.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the digital terrain model meets analysis requirements.
Validation of module interfaces and ownership boundaries to ensure no hidden diffusion or overlap.
